msgs.text:
I treated the Turn 9–17 dynamic tame-inertia word as the only still-live candidate. The prompt fixes the four-generator tame format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma = tau^2, pro-2-core generators after them, and exponents reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior file gives several decisive finite-quotient refutations of earlier candidates, so I did not regard those as viable.

The surviving candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with π=π2 and η=2π on tame inertia,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,where π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η=−2π ≡50932630783(modM).My new check is not another finite-tame cup-rank test. Instead I pushed the remaining Frobenius-action rigidity issue one Zassenhaus layer beyond Turn 17.Set τ=1 and pass to the unramified quadratic quotient. Since π≡1(mod2), the candidate specializes tor=xσ−1 xσ[σ,y].

Let U=ker(σ↦C2), S=σ2, and let α be conjugation by σ. In the degree-one Zassenhaus quotient use the Turn 16/17 basisa0=x0,a1=x1,b=y0+x1,c=S.

Then α swaps a0,a1 and fixes b,c. The marked generator is c=S. The Demuškin initial relation isq=a0[2]+a1[2]+[b,c].

Write R=F2[C2], the two-dimensional regular indecomposable, and T=F2, the trivial module. ThusL1=U1/U2≅R⊕Tb⊕Tc.

Turn 15 found a nontrivial ambiguity in L2, and Turn 17’s explicit D4 -descent calculation fixes the last residual U/U3 bit. I checked the next graded pieces of the restricted Lie algebra

L=gr(U)=n⨁Un/Un+1 as F2[C2]-modules. Using the free restricted Lie algebra on a0,a1,b,c modulo the restricted ideal generated by q, the PBW/Hilbert dimensions agree with the Demuškin series 1/(1−4t+t2), and row reduction gives:L3≅R8,so there is no new marked first-order ambiguity at the next step. But in degree four,L4≅R24⊕T6.Concretely, in degree four the free restricted Lie piece has dimension 70; the degree-four part of the ideal generated by q, including [q,L2], [[q,L1],L1], and q[2], has rank 16; hence dimL4=54. The operator N=α+1 has rank 24, so L4 decomposes as R24⊕T6.This matters because marked degree-four IA deformations of the Frobenius action are measured, to first order, byH1(C2,HomF2 (R⊕Tb,L4)),where c=S is excluded because the marked square S=σ2 must stay fixed. Since H1(C2,R)=0 and H1(C2,T)=F2, the six trivial summands in L4giveH1(C2,Hom(R⊕Tb,L4))≅F26.There is an explicit nonzero class. Letw=[a0,a1][2]∈L4.

In the quotient by the Demuškin relation, w=0, α(w)=w, and w∈/(α+1)L4. Therefore the degree-four deformation d(b)=w,d(a0)=d(a1)=d(c)=0 is a nontrivial cocycle modulo IA conjugacy. It fixes the marked element S, does not change the action on U/U4, and is invisible to the mod-2 cup product, the Bockstein/sign character, the abelianized mod-4 comparison, and the D4 commutator bit from Turn 17. It only first appears in the action on U/U5. It also preserves the Demuškin relation modulo degree five: changing b by a degree-four term changes [b,c] only in degree five.

So Turn 17’s arithmetic D4 -descent check is real and useful, but it does not close the formation gap. There is a fresh six-dimensional fourth-degree ambiguity in the marked unramified-quadratic Frobenius action. This does not refute the candidate; it shows that a proof of the candidate cannot stop at Demuškin type, orientation, U/U3, or the first D4 descent invariant.

ESTABLISHED: The live candidate is still the Turn 9–17 word. New this turn: in the unramified quadratic kernel, after the Turn 17 U/U3 bit is fixed, L3≅R8 gives no new ambiguity, but L4≅R24⊕T6 gives a six-dimensional marked Frobenius-action ambiguity on U/U5. An explicit class is d(b)=[a0,a1][2].

GAP: The candidate is not refuted, but the global dyadic formation proof is still incomplete. One must compute the candidate’s six degree-four Frobenius-action classes and match them with arithmetic Kummer/embedding-problem invariants; Turn 17’s D4 calculation only fixes the previous layer.

NEXT: Compute the actual degree-four IA class selected by the candidate in the unramified quadratic case, then identify the corresponding arithmetic invariant, likely a higher 2-central or Massey/Kummer embedding problem beyond the ordinary D4 descent.[BREAKTHROUGH]
